sections.content:
Now I can see the player sprite is at rows 37-40, cols 14-16 (after moving LEFT). The player is the yellow/blue figure.
The green blocks are in two rows:
- Row 1: rows 25-29, cols 14-16, 20-22, 26-28, 32-34, 38-40, 44-46, 50-52
- Row 2: rows 31-35, cols 14-16, 20-22, 26-28, 32-34, 38-40, 44-46, 50-52
The player is now at cols 14-16, which aligns with the leftmost green blocks.
Let me try clicking the center-bottom green block (the "dangerous" one) while the player is NOT aligned with it. The dangerous block is at cols 20-23 (approximately).
Actually, looking more carefully, the green blocks are at cols 14-16, 20-22, 26-28, etc. The "dangerous" one was at cols 20-23 in the previous layout. Let me check which one causes game_over.
Actually, I realize I need to rethink this. In the initial layout, there was only ONE green block at the bottom (cols 20-23). But now after the reset, there are 14 green blocks in a 2x7 grid. This is the INITIAL state of level 2.
So the initial state has 14 green blocks. The "dangerous" block I identified earlier was the center-bottom one at cols 20-23, which was the only remaining block after I cleared all others.
Let me try a new approach: move the player to a position NOT aligned with the center-bottom block, then clear all blocks including that one.
The player is currently at cols 14-16 (after moving LEFT). The center-bottom green block is at cols 20-22 (id=132). Let me try clicking it now.

# The dangerous block was at cols 20-23. Let me click it while player is NOT aligned.
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 33, 'col': 21}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
